finding: How decision-makers allocate scarce resources among competing principles depends on their role (dictator vs recipient) and the prior norms signaled by reciprocity cues; allocators shift weightings of accountability versus libertarian principles in response to conditions that signal fairness expectations.

The ordinary concept of weakness of will is not a simple disjunction of judgment-violation and resolution-violation, but a prototype combining multiple contributory factors; moral valence of the action independently affects confidence in attributions of weakness.

Preview benefit from word n+2 in reading occurs only when the intervening word (n+1) is extremely high frequency; low-frequency words in the n+1 position eliminate the n+2 preview effect, suggesting parafoveal processing capacity is constrained by intervening-word load.

Regional deterioration in maternal and child health services correlates strongly with regional AIDS prevalence; the divergence in health-service quality between high-AIDS and low-AIDS regions began in the mid-1990s, before major antiretroviral rollout, indicating supply-side disruption or resource diversion.

Partnership formation and dissolution have symmetric effects on subjective well-being; marriage increases well-being more than cohabitation, and this advantage is similar across same-sex and different-sex couples, though gender differences appear in same-sex partnerships and age-cohort effects differ for cohabitation.

When intentions and outcomes are held constant, harmful commissions are judged as more immoral than harmful omissions (omission bias); this bias correlates with perceived differences in causal role and is not fully explained by perceived intention, but can be reduced by making causal responsibility explicit.
